steps.title: Apply the vertical shift rule
steps.content:
When a function is shifted up by $k$ units, we add $k$ to the function value. For a shift up by 8 units, we have:
$$g(x) = f(x) + 8$$

steps.title: Check all choices
steps.content:
• "$g(x) = 4x + 7 + 8$": Equals $4x + 15$, which is $f(x)$ shifted up by 8 ✓
  • "$g(x) = 4x + 7 - 8$": Equals $4x - 1$, a shift down by 8, not up ✗
  • "$g(x) = 4x + 56$": Shifts $f(x)$ up by 49 units (from $4x+7$ to $4x+56$), not 8 units. Eliminated ✗
  • "$g(x) = 32x + 7$": Multiplies the slope by 8 instead of adding 8 to the output ✗
That matches the correct answer - A is correct.
